message.content:
Explore the Angular app at D:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend to map the existing **item detail** page so it can be redesigned. I need a thorough report. Investigate VERY thoroughly across multiple locations.

Find and report on:

1. **The itemDetail component**: Look under `src/app/pages/itemDetail/` (and anywhere similar like `item-detail`, `itemMaster`). Report the exact folder path and list its files (.ts, .html, .scss). For the component .ts file, quote: the @Component decorator (selector, templateUrl, styleUrls), the class properties (especially the item data model object and its field names), how it loads data (service calls, route params, ngOnInit), and the methods. Show me the real field names on the item object (e.g. itemNum, productType, etc.) so I can map the design's sample fields to real ones.

2. **The HTML template**: Summarize how it currently renders (~40 fields in flat rows across two sub-tabs per the design brief). Quote representative chunks showing the field bindings and the two sub-tab structure. Note what UI library is used (Angular Material? mat-tab? fxLayout? plain CSS grid?).

3. **Routing**: Find the route definition that maps to this component (e.g. `/apps/itemDetail/:id` or similar). Quote the route path and how the :id param is read.

4. **The data service & model**: Find the service that fetches the item and any TypeScript interface/model/class for the item. Quote the field names and types. I specifically need the real field names for: identity (item number, reference, art number, display name, description, division), merch-group codes (the MG01-MG15 fields — product type, sub-type, property, licensor, style guide, size, art source, artist, age group, for customer, etc.), specs (width, depth, weight, UOM, packaging, cost, lead time, vendor, note, season, product manager), status fields, dates, team/assignments, tags, attachments, comments.

5. **MG-code schema**: Search the codebase for "MG01", "MG02"... "MG15", "merchGroup", "merch_group", "mg_code" or similar. The design assumes MG01=productType, MG02=productSubType, MG03=productSubSubType, MG04=size, MG05=licensor, MG06=property, MG07=styleGuide, MG08=artSource, MG09=artist, MG10=ageGroup, MG15=forCustomer. Confirm or correct these against the real schema — report exactly what you find (field names, labels, any mapping table/config).

6. **Licensing & Calendar**: Find any existing licensing / license-tracking / license-stage-tracker component and any calendar/key-dates component related to items. Report paths and the data shape they consume.

7. **Styling/theming conventions**: How does this app do styling? SCSS with variables? A theme file? CSS custom properties already defined? Where is the global styles file? Is there an existing design-token/SCSS-variables file I should extend? What fonts are loaded (index.html / angular.json)? Report the global styles path and any existing color/spacing variables.

8. **Icon library**: What icons does the app use (mat-icon? a custom SVG set? font icons?). Report how icons are rendered so I can match the design's line-icon set.

9. **Component conventions**: standalone components vs NgModules? What Angular version (check package.json)? Are there shared/reusable UI components (cards, pills, badges) already? Where do shared components live?

Report concrete file paths (relative to designflow-frontend) and quote real field names and code. Do NOT modify anything. Be exhaustive — this drives a full page rebuild.

# Designflow ItemDetail Component Exploration Report ## 1. Component Location & Files **Path:** `src/app/pages/itemDetail/` **Files:** - `itemDetail.component.ts` (880 lines) - Main component class - `itemDetail.component.html` (1336 lines) - Template with 3 nested tabs + licensing/calendar tabs - `itemDetail.component.scss` (355 lines) - Styling with Material Design and custom layouts - `itemDetail-routing.module.ts` - Route definition - `itemDetail.module.ts` - NgModule declarations --- ## 2. The ItemDetail Component: TypeScript Class **@Component Decorator:** ```typescript @Component({ selector: "app-itemDetail", templateUrl: "./itemDetail.component.html", styleUrls: ["./itemDetail.component.scss"], standalone: false // Using NgModule, not standalone }) ``` **Route Path:** `/apps/itemDetail/:item` (from app-routing.module.ts line 177) - The `:item` parameter is read from the URL as `itemDetailId` via `document.location.href.split("/").pop()` **Key Class Properties (Data Model):** The core item data object is `itemData: any = []` (line 101, indexed as `itemData[0]` when loaded). **Real Field Names on Item Object:** ```typescript // Identity Fields item_num_id // Style number (key identifier) ref_num // Reference number (when no style number) dsn_ref_num // Art number / design reference art_piece_id // Link to art-piece component item_displ_descr_name // Display description (shown in header) item_descr_name // Item description (editable) item_content // Item content field div_code_fk // Division code (1=POP Lic, 2/9=Spruce, 7=POP Lic, 8=Spruce Lic) // MG Code Fields (Merchandise Group - see section 5 for mappings) udf_merchgroup01 // Product Type (MG01) udf_merchgroup01_id // Product Type ID udf_merchgroup02 // Product Sub-Type (MG02) udf_merchgroup02_id // Product Sub-Type ID udf_merchgroup03 // Product Sub-Sub-Type (MG03) udf_merchgroup03_id // Product Sub-Sub-Type ID udf_merchgroup04 // Size (MG04) udf_merchgroup04_id // Size ID udf_merchgroup05_fk // Licensor (MG05) OR Big Theme (if div_code=9) udf_merchgroup05_fk_id // Licensor/Big Theme ID udf_merchgroup06_fk // Property (MG06) OR Little Theme (if div_code=9) udf_merchgroup06_fk_id // Property/Little Theme ID udf_merchgroup07_fk // Style Guide (MG07) OR Art Type (if div_code=9) udf_merchgroup08_fk // Art Source (MG08) udf_merchgroup08_fk_id // Art Source ID udf_merchgroup09_fk // Artist (MG09) udf_merchgroup09_fk_id // Artist ID udf_merchgroup10_fk // Age Group (MG10) udf_merchgroup10_fk_id // Age Group ID udf_merchgroup15_fk // For Customer (MG15) - stored as customer name udf_merchgroup15_fk_id // For Customer ID udf_merchgroup11_fk // Style Guide alternatives array // Spec & Packaging Fields item_width_size // Item width item_depth_size // Item depth (selected from depthOptions) item_weight_size // Item weight item_note // Item note / specs note uom_size_fk // Measurement UOM uom_weight_fk // Weight UOM item_cost_ext // Standard cost mfg_lead_time // Manufacturing lead time vendor_code_fk // Vendor foreign key udf_freeform_06 // Packaging type (dropdown of predefined values) // Status Fields item_active_status // Active status (Y/N) discont_status // Discontinued status (Y/N) season_code_fk // Season code productmanager // Product manager name salesper_code_fk // Salesperson code (currently hardcoded "Adam") // Date Fields (mapped to dates[] array) udf_date01 // Earliest on Shelf date udf_date02 // Test Report Recv Date udf_date03 // Photography Recv Date udf_date05 // Fty Sent for Testing udf_date06 // Fty Sent for Photo created_time_date // Created time (index [5] in dates array) due_date // Due date (index [6]) // Additional Fields tags // CSV string of keyword tags (split/joined in component) udf_freeform_02 // Production status (displayed in header) udf_freeform_03 // Brand Assurance udf_freeform_05 // Cust Designed field udf_freeform_07 // Mold Paid Inv udf_freeform_08 // Contr Smpl PO udf_freeform_09 // Annu Smpl PO udf_item_priceA, B, C, D // Item prices (pricing tab) selling_price // Selling price retail_price // Retail price carton_length_size // Carton dimensions carton_width_size carton_depth_size carton_weight_size innerpack_qty // Inner pack quantity udf_int01, udf_int02 // UDF integer fields udfnum01 // UDF numeric field hts_num_ext_fk // HTS number royalty_code_fk // Royalty code mod_user_fk // Modified by user is_item_old // Flag: whether item is old (affects MG selection logic) productUserAssignments // Array of product team assignments // Images & Attachments itemAttachments // Array of file attachments ``` **Component Initialization (ngAfterViewInit/ngOnInit):** ```typescript async ngOnInit() { // 1. Get item ID from URL: /apps/itemDetail/:item this.itemDetailId = href[href.length - 1].split('?')[0] // 2. Load division code (calls getDivisionCode() which fetches itemData) const divisionCode = await this.getDivisionCode() // 3. Load MG codes grouped by type (Material, Construction, Feature, Size, Licensor, // Property, Big Theme, Little Theme, Style guide, Art Type, Art Source, Artist, Age Group) await this.itemService.getMerchGroup({ divisionCode }) // 4. Load item depth options, seasonal codes, company codes, vendors, UDF elements await this.getItemDepth() // 5. Call getItemInfo() to populate all dropdowns with current values await this.getItemInfo() // 6. Set up async tabs array (Main tab + Licensing (if not div 9) + Calendar) } ``` **Key Methods:** - `getDivisionCode()` - Fetches itemData from ItemDetailService via `getItemInfo({ id })` - `getItemInfo()` - Populates all selected* properties by matching item data to loaded options - `onSave()` - Marshals form values back to itemData object and calls `ItemDetailService.updateData()` - `onStyleNumber()` - Requests a new style number via `ItemDetailService.reqStyeNumber()` - `onProductAssignmentChange(role, event)` - Saves team member assignments - `loadProductAssignmentUsers()` - Fetches all users for product team dropdowns --- ## 3. HTML Template Structure **Layout Overview:** The template uses Angular Material (`mat-*`) components with `fxLayout` (FlexLayout) for responsive grid layouts. **Header Card (Top):** ```html Style #: {{data.item_num_id}} ``` **Main Tab Group (mat-tab-group with 3 nested content tabs):** **Tab 0 - "First" (Main Merchandising Tab):** - Read-only view: Displays ~18 fields in flex-wrap grid (2 columns per row) - Edit view: Same fields as mat-form-field inputs + editable tags - Fields shown: Active Status, Item Description, Product Type (MG01), Property (MG06), Product Sub-Type (MG02), Style Guide (MG07), Product Sub-Sub-Type (MG03), Item Content, Size (MG04), Brand Assurance, Season, Product Manager, Licensor (MG05), For Customer (MG15), Art Number reference **Tab 1 - "Second" (Specs & Dates Tab):** - Read-only view: Displays ~24 spec/date fields - Edit view: Inputs for dimensions, weights, dates (with mat-datepicker), vendor selection - Fields: Active Status, Discontinued Status, Item Width/Depth/Weight, UOMs, Item Note, Art Source (MG08), Packaging Type, Artist (MG09), Standard Cost, Age Group (MG10), Date fields (Earliest on Shelf, Due Date, Test Report, Photography, Testing/Photo Send), Mfg Lead Time, Vendor, Art Number **Tab 2 - "Third" (Pricing & Logistics Tab):** - Pricing: Item Prices A/B/C/D, Selling Price, Retail Price - Carton dimensions and weight, Inner pack qty - HTS Number, Royalty Code, Sales Person, Mold Paid Inv, various UDF fields, Created Time, Mod User **Two-Row Header Sub-Components (Right of main image):** 1. **Comments Card** - `` component 2. **Tech Pack Card** - Upload/View techpack files 3. **Tags Card** - Displays tags as chips (if not editing), or as editable chip-grid with ENTER to add 4. **Product Team Card** - 5 role dropdowns (Creative Designer, Creative Director, Technical Designer, Licensing, Salesperson) **Tabs 1+ (Conditional Tabs):** - **Licensing Tab** (tab.id='1'): Shows `` component (if div_code != 9) - **Calendar Tab** (tab.id='2'): Shows `` component with key dates **Edit/Save Buttons:** - Read-only: "Edit" button + "Request a style number" / "Update Item Details" button - Edit mode: "Save" and "Close" buttons --- ## 4. Data Service & Model **Service File:** `src/app/helpers/services/item_detail.service.ts` **Key Methods:** ```typescript getItemInfo(id: any) // POST /detail/getItemInfo updateData(data: any) // POST /detail/updateData reqStyeNumber(data: any) // POST /detail/reqStyeNumber getComments(id: number) // GET /detail/getComments/:id addComment(comment: CommentInterface) // POST /detail/addComment UpdateLicensingStatus(data) // POST /detail/UpdateLicensingStatus GetLicensingStatus(id) // GET /detail/GetLicensingStatus/:id getProductAssignments(itemId) // GET /detail/assignments/:itemId setProductAssignments(itemId, assignments) // POST /detail/assignments/:itemId ``` **Type Definitions:** ```typescript export type ProductAssignmentRole = | 'creative_designer' | 'creative_director' | 'technical_designer' | 'licensing_person' | 'salesperson'; export interface ProductAssignmentUser { id: number; name: string; lastname: string; email: string; profile_photo?: string | null; level?: string; } export interface ProductAssignment { id: number; item_id_fk: number; role: ProductAssignmentRole; user_id_fk: number; user?: ProductAssignmentUser; } ``` **Main Service:** `src/app/helpers/services/main.service.ts` **Key Methods for ItemDetail:** ```typescript getMerchGroup(data: { divisionCode }) // POST /getMerchGroup getSeasonCode() // GET /getSeasonCode getCompanyCode() // GET /getCompanyCode getAllVendors() // GET /getAllVendors getItemDepth() // GET /findItemDepth getCustomers() // GET /findCustomers getUDFElements(data) // POST /getUDFElement ``` --- ## 5. MG-Code (Merchandise Group) Schema **Real Schema Mapping (NOT as assumed in design brief):** The actual code loads MG codes from the backend grouped by **mgTypeDesc** (not by numeric code). The component maps them to the following logical purposes: | Display Label | Field Name | Backend mgTypeDesc | Component Property | Notes | |---|---|---|---|---| | Product Type | udf_merchgroup01 | "Material" | selectedProductType | MG01 logically | | Product Sub-Type | udf_merchgroup02 | "Construction" | selectedProductSubType | MG02 logically | | Product Sub-Sub-Type | udf_merchgroup03 | "Feature" | selectedProductSubSubType | MG03 logically | | Size | udf_merchgroup04 | "Size" | selectedSize | MG04 logically | | Licensor (non-Spruce9) | udf_merchgroup05_fk | "Licensor" | selectedLicensor | MG05 logically | | Big Theme (Spruce9 only) | udf_merchgroup05_fk | "Big Theme" | selectedBigTheme | MG05 alternative for div=9 | | Property (non-Spruce9) | udf_merchgroup06_fk | "Property" | selectedProperty | MG06 logically | | Little Theme (Spruce9 only) | udf_merchgroup06_fk | "Little Theme" | selectedLittleTheme | MG06 alternative for div=9 | | Style Guide (non-Spruce9) | udf_merchgroup07_fk | "Style guide" | selectedStyleGuide | MG07 logically | | Art Type (Spruce9 only) | udf_merchgroup07_fk | "Art Type" | selectedArtType | MG07 alternative for div=9 | | Art Source | udf_merchgroup08_fk | "Art Source" | selectedArtSource | MG08 logically | | Artist | udf_merchgroup09_fk | "Artist" | selectedArtist | MG09 logically | | Age Group | udf_merchgroup10_fk | "Age Group" | selectedAgeGroup | MG10 logically | | For Customer | udf_merchgroup15_fk | (custom/customer table) | selectedForCustomer | MG15 logically | **Database Field Structure for Each MG:** ```typescript { mg_id: number, // Primary key mg_code: string, // Short code (used in dropdowns) mgCode2: string, // Alternative code (displayed in dropdowns) mg_desc: string, // Full description/title is_active: boolean, // Active flag parent_id?: number, // Parent category ID divisionCode_id_fk: number, // Division FK (for filtering by division) createdTime: Date, // Created timestamp (year extracted for old-item logic) mgTypeDesc: string // Type descriptor (Material, Construction, etc.) } ``` **Division-Specific Behavior:** - **Division 9 (Spruce non-Lic)**: Uses Big Theme, Little Theme, Art Type fields instead of Licensor, Property, Style Guide - **Other divisions**: Use standard Licensor, Property, Style Guide fields **Old Item Logic (lines 500-507):** - If `is_item_old === true`, MG selection matches by `mg_code` instead of `id` - Filters to inactive items NOT created in 2025 - Allows showing historical MG codes no longer in the active list --- ## 6. Licensing & Calendar Components **Licensing Component:** - **Path:** `src/app/pages/itemDetail/license-tracking/` - **Files:** - `license-tracking.component.ts` - `licensing-stage-tracker/licensing-stage-tracker.component.ts` - Tracks phases (Submission, Resubmission, PPS Approval) - `licensing-stage-tracker/licensing-stage-tracker.component.html` - `licensing_tracking_button/licensing_tracking_button.component.ts` - `feedback_dialog/feedback_dialog.component.ts` **Licensing Stage Tracker Data Shape:** ```typescript interface LicensingStageRow { phase: string; // 'Submission', 'Resubmission', 'PPS Approval' status?: string | null; // 'Pending', 'Approved', 'Rejected', 'In Revision' date?: string | null; // Actual date completionDate?: string | null; // When completed estStartDate?: string | null; // Estimated start estCompletionDate?: string | null; // Estimated completion neededDate?: string | null; // Needed by detailLines?: string[]; // Additional detail events?: LicensingStageEvent[]; // History of events per phase isDelayed?: boolean; // Delay flag } ``` **Calendar Component:** - **Path:** `src/app/pages/calendar/calendar.component.ts` - Uses `angular-calendar` library (v0.29.0) - Integrated into itemDetail as tab (id='2') - Shows key dates for the item --- ## 7. Styling & Theming Conventions **Global Styles File:** `src/styles.scss` **Fonts Loaded (index.html):** - Material Icons: `https://fonts.googleapis.com/icon?family=Material+Icons` - Material Symbols Outlined: `https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Material+Symbols+Outlined` - Inter (300, 400, 500, 600, 700): `https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter:wght@300;400;500;600;700` - Font Awesome 5.15.4: CDN link - PrimeIcons 7.0.0: CDN link **CSS Variables/Theming:** - Uses CSS custom properties for theming (referenced as `rgb(var(--color-primary))`) - Angular Material theme imported from `@vex/styles/core` - Tailwind CSS configured via `src/@vex/styles/tailwind.scss` **ItemDetail Component SCSS Variables (Custom):** ```scss .flex-field // flex: 1 1 calc(50% - 16px) — two-column layout .full-width // width: 100% .read-only-container // flex layout, centered, column direction .read-only-field // flex column for label/value pairs .read-only-label // font-weight: 600, color: #424242, font-size: 14px .read-only-value // font-size: 16px, color: #616161 .custom-tab-group // Custom border styling, uses CSS vars .design-only-overlay // Absolute positioned, rotated text overlay .info-icon // Blue (#5C77FF), 16x16px, inline-flex .mat-icon-sm // 16x20px for small Material Icons .dropdown-div // 2-column dropdown with code/description .custom-card // Border-radius 16px, shadow, hover transform .product-assignments-card // Grid layout with auto-fit minmax(190px) ``` **Color Scheme:** - Primary color: CSS var `--color-primary` (blue-ish, #1976d2 seen in links) - Background: White with subtle grays (#f7f9fc, #e2e6ea) - Text: Dark grays (#424242 labels, #616161 values) - Info icons: Bright blue (#5C77FF) **Responsive Breakpoints (styles.scss):** - `@media (min-width: 950px)` - Desktop layout with side-by-side image + cards - `@media (max-width: 950px)` - Mobile/tablet stacking **Global SCSS Mixins:** ```scss @mixin page-component-layout { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 10px; } ``` --- ## 8. Icon Library **Icon System:** Material Icons (mat-icon) - Uses Google Material Icons font loaded in index.html - Also includes Material Symbols Outlined as fallback **Usage in ItemDetail:** ```html info close cancel ``` **No custom SVG icon set found** — relies entirely on Material Icons library. --- ## 9. Component Conventions & Architecture **Angular Version:** 21.1.3 (latest) **Component Type:** Traditional NgModule-based (not standalone) ```typescript // itemDetail.module.ts declares the component in an NgModule @NgModule({ declarations: [ItemDetailComponent, ...], imports: [MatCardModule, MatTabsModule, ...], providers: [TrackingService, AdminService, LicensingTrackingService] }) ``` **UI Library:** Angular Material 21.1.3 with: - `mat-card` for containers - `mat-tab-group` / `mat-tab` for tabbed sections - `mat-form-field` with `matInput` for text inputs - `mat-select` for dropdowns - `mat-datepicker` for date selection - `mat-chips` / `mat-chip-grid` for tags - `mat-icon` for icons - `mat-button` for buttons - FlexLayout (`@angular/flex-layout` v15.0.0-beta.42) for responsive layouts **Layout System:** - Uses `fxLayout="row wrap"` and `fxLayoutGap="16px"` for flex grids - Fallback CSS Grid in `.tracking` card (grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr)) - No bootstrap or custom CSS framework — pure Angular Material + Flex Layout **Data Binding:** - Two-way binding: `[(ngModel)]="itemData[0].field_name"` on inputs - Template interpolation: `{{ data.field_name | date }}` - Conditional rendering: `@if` and `@for` (Angular 17+ syntax) **Form Handling:** - Uses `FormsModule` ([(ngModel)]) - Uses `ReactiveFormsModule` for date controls with `[formControl]="dates[index]"` - No form-level validation visible in current code **Shared Components Imported:** - `` - Custom image uploader - `` - Comments/notes dialog - `` - Licensing tracker - `` - Calendar for key dates - `` - Progress tracker **Services:** - `ItemDetailService` - Item CRUD operations - `itemService` (main.service) - Global business data (MG codes, vendors, etc.) - `AdminService` - Admin operations - `LicensingTrackingService` - Licensing operations - `LoaderService` - Global loading indicator --- ## 10. Key Observations for Design Rebuild 1. **Data Complexity:** The item object has ~60+ fields spread across 3 content tabs + 2 async tabs (Licensing, Calendar). The design must accommodate all fields in an intuitive hierarchy. 2. **Division-Based Conditionals:** The UI changes significantly based on `div_code_fk` (line 9 = Spruce non-Lic shows different MG fields). Your design must account for this branching. 3. **Edit Mode Toggle:** The entire form switches between read-only and editable views. Both views must coexist in the template (currently uses `@if (!editing)` / `@else`). 4. **Nested Tabs:** Main content uses mat-tab-group with 3 tabs ("First", "Second", "Third"), then the outer component has async tabs (Main, Licensing, Calendar). The nesting is 2 levels deep. 5. **Field Names:** Use the exact `udf_*` and specific field names listed in Section 2. The "MG01-MG15" are conceptual labels; the real fields are `udf_merchgroup01`, `udf_merchgroup05_fk`, etc. 6. **MG Code Loading:** The backend returns a nested structure: `data[].merchGroupHeaders[].merchGroups[]`. The component loops through all and groups by `mgTypeDesc`. Your design doesn't need to know this; just bind to the dropdown options arrays. 7. **Flexbox + Grid Hybrid:** Current layout uses both `fxLayout` (flex-layout directive) for rows and CSS Grid for the tracking header. Use Material + Flex Layout for consistency. 8. **Responsive:** Two breakpoints at 950px width. Your redesign should maintain this or improve mobile UX. 9. **Tags as CSV:** Tags are stored as CSV string (`tags.join(',')`) and split on load. Component handles the edit chip-grid automatically. 10. **Licensing Status:** Fetched from a separate endpoint and displayed as a badge in the header. The licensing tab shows a stage-tracker component with phases. --- ## File Paths Summary | Purpose | Path | |---------|------| | Component | `src/app/pages/itemDetail/itemDetail.component.ts` | | Template | `src/app/pages/itemDetail/itemDetail.component.html` | | Styles | `src/app/pages/itemDetail/itemDetail.component.scss` | | Module | `src/app/pages/itemDetail/itemDetail.module.ts` | | Service | `src/app/helpers/services/item_detail.service.ts` | | Main Service | `src/app/helpers/services/main.service.ts` | | Licensing Service | `src/app/helpers/services/licensing_tracking.service.ts` | | Licensing Tracker | `src/app/pages/itemDetail/license-tracking/licensing-stage-tracker/licensing-stage-tracker.component.ts` | | Calendar | `src/app/pages/calendar/calendar.component.ts` | | Global Styles | `src/styles.scss` | | Tailwind Config | `src/@vex/styles/tailwind.scss` | | Vex Theme | `src/@vex/styles/core` | --- You now have a complete map of the itemDetail page structure, all real field names, the MG code schema, routing, services, styling infrastructure, and UI conventions. This should provide everything needed to redesign the page comprehensively.
